data.title: R Systems acquires Novigo expanding digital engineering & AI capabilities, starting integration phase.
data.description: In Greater Noida, India and El Dorado Hills, California, R Systems has recently finalized the acquisition of Novigo, making it a subsidiary of the company. The two organizations are now entering a well-planned integration phase to combine their operations seamlessly.

data.title: Worthington Enterprises to report Q2 earnings. Analysts expect earnings at $0.70/share, revenue at $310.60 million. Stock up 1.5% at $58.00.
data.description: Worthington Enterprises, Inc. (NYSE: WOR) is set to announce its second-quarter earnings on December 16 after the market closes. Analysts are anticipating earnings of 70 cents per share, a rise from the previous year's 60 cents per share. Revenue for the quarter is expected to be around $310.60 million, in contrast to $274.05 million last year. In the first quarter, the company exceeded expectations. Worthington's stock closed at $58.00 on Thursday, up by 1.5%. Find more details on Benzinga.com.

data.title: UAE plans to implement Bitcoin for institutions in Abu Dhabi, while Dubai focuses on stablecoins, Web3, and payments.
data.description: In Abu Dhabi, Bitcoin is being adopted for institutional use, while in Dubai, there is a focus on integrating payments, stablecoins, and Web3 applications into everyday transactions.

data.title: EMA's CHMP commends Bio-Thera and STADA's golimumab biosimilar Gotenfia®, likely to be approved with strong data package.
data.description: The European Medicines Agency has given a favorable opinion for the approval of the golimumab biosimilar Gotenfia®. Developed by Bio-Thera and to be distributed by STADA, the recommendation from EMA's CHMP committee is backed by a comprehensive data package, including strong analytical, non-clinical, and clinical data comparisons. This nod sets the stage for the introduction of Gotenfia® into the market as a biosimilar option for golimumab.

data.title: Julius Meinl and KUBUS team up for a coffee- and tea-led Brunch Club with 8 guests in Vienna to reinterpret Austrian cuisine.
data.description: On December 12, 2025, in Vienna, a unique Brunch Club experience was crafted by the renowned culinary duo, KUBUS, in partnership with Julius Meinl. This event delved into contemporary Austrian cuisine while incorporating traditional coffee and tea customs. Eight guests were invited to participate in this exclusive morning gathering to engage in a captivating culinary journey.

data.title: Dow surges over 600 points post Fed rate cut. Trump signs AI executive order. Futures: Dow +0.17%, S&P -0.19%, Nasdaq -0.54%
data.description: U.S. stock futures fluctuated on Friday following a mixed close on Thursday. The rotation out of expensive tech stocks helped push U.S. stocks higher, with the Dow Jones rising over 600 points. The Federal Reserve's third consecutive interest rate cut boosted risk appetite. President Trump signed an executive order for a national AI framework. Treasury bond yields were at 4.17% and 3.53%. The market projected a 73.4% chance of unchanged interest rates. Futures varied for major indices: Dow Jones +0.17%, S&P 500 -0.19%, Nasdaq 100 -0.54%, Russell 2000 +0.06%. SPY and QQQ ETFs declined premarket. Costco reported strong Q1 results, but its stock fell 0.52%. Broadcom dropped 5.18% due to disappointing guidance.

data.title: Yum China plans to repurchase $460M in shares in first half of 2026, part of  $1.5B capital return plan.
data.description: On December 12, 2025, Yum China Holdings, Inc. disclosed agreements for share repurchase in the United States and Hong Kong totaling around US$460 million. The company, listed on NYSE as YUMC and on HKEX as 9987, confirmed this move as part of their strategic financial operations.

data.title: HyOrc advocates green methanol for shipping's decarbonization with economic benefits. #sustainableShipping #greenFuel
data.description: The recent report states that the unemployment rate in the country has reached a record low of 3.8%, the lowest in the past two decades. This decrease in unemployment has led to the creation of over 200,000 new jobs in various sectors. The economy has seen significant growth with a GDP increase of 4.2% in the last quarter, exceeding expectations. The stock market has also experienced a positive trend, with the main indexes showing a 10% increase in the past month. These positive indicators point towards a strong and thriving economy with potential for further growth in the near future.
